en.extract:
The long-billed honeyeater (Melilestes megarhynchus) is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is monotypic within the genus Melilestes.


Distribution and habitat
It is found in New Guinea and offshore islands, where its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest. Long-billed honeyeaters do not migrate. The average generation length of it is 5.8 years.

The '''long-billed honeyeater''' ('''''Melilestes megarhynchus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]]. It is [[monotypic]] within the genus '''''Melilestes'''''.<ref name = "ITIS"/>

==Distribution and habitat==
It is found in [[New Guinea]] and offshore islands,<ref name="iucn status 13 November 2021" /> where its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]].<ref name="iucn status 13 November 2021" /> Long-billed honeyeaters do not [[Bird migration|migrate]].<ref name="iucn status 13 November 2021" /> The average [[Generation time|generation length]] of it is 5.8 years.<ref name="iucn status 13 November 2021" />
